content.problemStatement:
XXX and YYY are binary sequences of length NNN, i.e. 0≤Xi,Yi≤10 \le X_i, Y_i \le 10≤Xi​,Yi​≤1. There are 444 positive integer parameters AAA, BBB, CCC, and DDD.

You can do the following set of operations any number of times:


Choose 222 indices iii and jjj such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i < j \le N1≤i<j≤N.
Perform **exactly** one of the following actions:

Swap XiX_iXi​ and XjX_jXj​, paying a cost of AAA if Xi=1X_i = 1Xi​=1 (after the swap) and otherwise a cost of BBB.
Swap YiY_iYi​ and YjY_jYj​, paying a cost of CCC if Yi=1Y_i = 1Yi​=1 (after the swap) and otherwise a cost of DDD.


Define f(X,Y)f(X, Y)f(X,Y) as the minimum cost to make the sequences XXX and YYY equal. If it is not possible to make them equal, f(X,Y)=0f(X, Y) = 0f(X,Y)=0.

Given NNN, AAA, BBB, CCC and DDD, consider the 4N4^N4N pairs of binary sequences XXX and YYY (both of length NNN). Find the sum of f(X,Y)f(X, Y)f(X,Y) over all such pairs.

Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains 555 integers - NNN, AAA, BBB, CCC and DDD.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of f(X,Y)f(X, Y)f(X,Y) over all 4N4^N4N pairs of binary sequences of length NNN modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 400400400.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are 444 possible pairs of sequences:

X=(0),Y=(0)X = (0), Y = (0)X=(0),Y=(0) : X=YX = YX=Y already, so f(X,Y)=0f(X, Y) = 0f(X,Y)=0
X=(1),Y=(0)X = (1), Y = (0)X=(1),Y=(0) : XXX cannot be made equal to YYY, so f(X,Y)=0f(X, Y) = 0f(X,Y)=0
X=(0),Y=(1)X = (0), Y = (1)X=(0),Y=(1) : XXX cannot be made equal to YYY, so f(X,Y)=0f(X, Y) = 0f(X,Y)=0
X=(1),Y=(1)X = (1), Y = (1)X=(1),Y=(1) : XXX is already equal to YYY, so f(X,Y)=0f(X, Y) = 0f(X,Y)=0

Test Case 2 : There are 161616 total pairs of sequences. Out of that, only 222 pairs have a positive value:

X=(0,1),Y=(1,0)X = (0, 1), Y = (1, 0)X=(0,1),Y=(1,0) : Choose i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2 and do the first action, swap X1X_1X1​ and X2X_2X2​, paying a cost of A=1A = 1A=1. f(X,Y)=1f(X, Y) = 1f(X,Y)=1.
X=(1,0),Y=(0,1)X = (1, 0), Y = (0, 1)X=(1,0),Y=(0,1) : Choose i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2 and do the first action, swap X1X_1X1​ and X2X_2X2​, paying a cost of B=1B = 1B=1. f(X,Y)=1f(X, Y) = 1f(X,Y)=1.

Thus, the sum is 222.
